status_definitions.supported: Executable or validated for the documented subset, with regression evidence.
status_definitions.partial: Some forms are executable or validated, but important COBOL forms remain blocked or under-tested.
status_definitions.blocked: Detected and rejected before generated Rust is emitted, or intentionally unavailable.
status_definitions.unknown: Not yet assessed with enough source, semantic, runtime, or oracle evidence.

policy.granularity: Feature groups tracked by the converter, VM, record tooling, and ABYSS hazard matrix. This is not a clause-by-clause ISO certification list.

features.name: Fixed source format
features.notes: Basic fixed normalization is covered; fixed continuation and copybook edge cases remain risk areas.

features.name: Free source format
features.notes: Used by the converter smoke and oracle suites for supported procedure subsets.

features.name: Automatic source-format detection
features.notes: Recognizes free-format directives and indented free-form division headers; still not a substitute for explicit source format in migration runs.

features.name: Local COPY expansion
features.notes: Local copybooks with recursion/depth limits are supported for the covered subset.

features.name: COPY REPLACING pseudo-text pairs
features.notes: Simple pseudo-text replacement outside literals is supported; complex compiler semantics remain partial.

features.name: Fixed-format COPY continuation
features.notes: Needs stronger oracle coverage for literal and pseudo-text continuation.

features.name: Nested COPY directives and full compiler COPY semantics
features.notes: Full copybook compiler semantics are outside the current importer/converter subset.

features.name: IDENTIFICATION / ENVIRONMENT / DATA / PROCEDURE divisions
features.notes: Common division/section shapes are parsed; malformed non-program input and grammar completeness remain audit targets.

features.name: Sentence and period scope
features.notes: Token-slice typed imperative-list parsing is used for supported top-level procedure sentences and scoped file, sort, text, size-error, EVALUATE, and SEARCH branches. Procedure CFG report blocks preserve COBOL sentence/period scope and record NEXT SENTENCE targets; generated execution and remaining period-scope edge cases stay blocked.

features.name: Nested imperative statement lists
features.notes: Supported in selected IF, file, sort, text, COMPUTE size-error, EVALUATE, and SEARCH branches using spanned token slices rather than raw statement reparsing. Typed file I/O lowering no longer has legacy raw semantic IR variants; broader grammar gaps remain partial.

features.name: WORKING-STORAGE SECTION
features.notes: Byte-backed storage is generated for supported data categories.

features.name: LOCAL-STORAGE SECTION
features.notes: Lifecycle coverage exists for multi-program cases, but area-scoped layout remains a sensitive surface.

features.name: LINKAGE SECTION
features.notes: Structural linkage for linked CALL targets is supported; byte-compatible arbitrary linkage is not complete.

features.name: FD record areas
features.notes: Fixed-length sequential record areas are supported; variable-length and ODO FD records remain blocked.

features.name: SD sort record areas
features.notes: Fixed-length SD records are supported for procedure-based in-memory SORT.

features.name: Group items
features.notes: Group layout and group moves are supported for covered storage shapes.

features.name: Elementary PICTURE clauses
features.notes: Common PIC X/9/S/V forms are supported; edited, national, DBCS, and dialect edge cases remain partial or blocked.

features.name: FILLER items
features.notes: Repeated FILLER storage is modeled.

features.name: VALUE initialization
features.notes: Scalar initialization is covered; VALUE ranges, edited values, and dialect-sensitive initialization remain partial.

features.name: Level 66 RENAMES
features.notes: RENAMES remains blocked for converter execution.

features.name: Level 77 items
features.notes: Needs explicit parser/sema/runtime coverage.

features.name: Level 78 constants
features.notes: Audited as a gap; no migration-capable status yet.

features.name: Level 88 condition names
features.notes: Condition-name IF and SET are covered for scalar views; complex ranges and aliases need more oracle tests.

features.name: Fixed OCCURS tables
features.notes: Subscripts, indexes, and SEARCH use supported table metadata; all nested/dialect cases are not complete.

features.name: OCCURS DEPENDING ON
features.notes: Binary record tooling and selected VM ODO paths are covered; FD ODO and some dynamic layout references block.

features.name: REDEFINES overlays
features.notes: Record tooling and scoped metadata exist; procedure references through ambiguous overlays remain guarded or blocked.

features.name: SAME RECORD AREA
features.notes: Selected shared record area behavior is executable; full inter-file and SD aliasing chaos needs more oracle coverage.

features.name: GLOBAL and COMMON data
features.notes: COMMON program lifecycle exists; GLOBAL data clauses are blocked in ABYSS fixtures.

features.name: EXTERNAL storage
features.notes: Shared EXTERNAL storage is covered for linked generated programs.

features.name: DISPLAY alphanumeric
features.notes: Byte-backed alphanumeric moves and DISPLAY are covered.

features.name: DISPLAY numeric
features.notes: Common display numeric paths are supported; implied scale, overpunch, invalid numeric policy, and EBCDIC zoned cases require more work.

features.name: BINARY / COMP / COMP-4
features.notes: Record codecs and generated accessors exist; dialect-specific width/endian/truncation policy remains partial.

features.name: COMP-3 packed decimal
features.notes: Core packed decimal codec is mature. Generated programs support fixed scalar COMP-3 decode/display, strict MOVE/COMPUTE writes with size-error protection, and numeric SORT key ordering; broader comparison and dialect invalid-data policy coverage remains narrower.

features.name: COMP-5 native binary
features.notes: Schema support exists with explicit endian; COBOL compiler-native behavior needs dialect profiling.

features.name: COMP-1 / COMP-2 IBM hexadecimal floats
features.notes: Record codec support exists; generated procedure arithmetic is not complete.

features.name: NATIONAL and DBCS
features.notes: Detected as outside the current executable subset.

features.name: POINTER and PROCEDURE-POINTER
features.notes: Pointer storage and procedure pointer calls are blocked.

features.name: SIGN IS SEPARATE
features.notes: Blocked until display numeric codec policy is complete.

features.name: JUSTIFIED
features.notes: Blocked in executable converter subset.

features.name: BLANK WHEN ZERO
features.notes: Blocked in executable converter subset.

features.name: SYNC alignment
features.notes: Sequential planning supports alignment slack; dialect-specific layout needs more oracle evidence.

features.name: SELECT file declarations
features.notes: Common SELECT forms are parsed; OPTIONAL and dynamic clauses need stricter coverage.

features.name: ASSIGN TO literal
features.notes: Literal ASSIGN fallback, legacy generated file maps, and structured runtime config are supported for fixed sequential files.

features.name: ASSIGN TO identifier
features.notes: Dynamic ASSIGN is detected and blocks executable generation; runtime platform config does not unlock dynamic ASSIGN.

features.name: FILE STATUS
features.notes: Status binding and common sequential statuses are covered; dialect-specific ranges need oracle expansion.

features.name: ORGANIZATION SEQUENTIAL fixed-length files
features.notes: OS-backed fixed-length sequential files are executable through literal ASSIGN, legacy file maps, or structured runtime config. Runtime config rejects duplicate names, unsupported encodings, and disposition/open-mode mismatches.

features.name: LINE SEQUENTIAL files
features.notes: Detected and blocked; structured runtime config is limited to fixed-length sequential records.

features.name: INDEXED / VSAM files
features.notes: Indexed and VSAM configs parse but fail validation; random/dynamic access, START, and key semantics are not implemented.

features.name: RELATIVE files
features.notes: Relative configs parse but fail validation; relative organization remains outside the current subset.

features.name: OPEN and CLOSE
features.notes: Input/output/extend coverage uses typed procedure AST lowering; I-O and platform-specific modes remain narrower.

features.name: READ with AT END / NOT AT END
features.notes: Sequential fixed-length READ and typed AT END / NOT AT END / ON EXCEPTION branch bodies are covered. Legacy raw READ semantic IR has been removed.

features.name: WRITE
features.notes: Fixed sequential WRITE uses typed procedure AST lowering; printer/report control behavior remains partial.

features.name: REWRITE
features.notes: Implemented for current fixed sequential mutation slice with typed INVALID KEY / NOT INVALID KEY branches. Legacy raw REWRITE semantic IR has been removed; last-record edge cases need more oracle coverage.

features.name: DELETE
features.notes: Implemented for current file mutation slice with typed INVALID KEY / NOT INVALID KEY branches. Legacy raw DELETE semantic IR has been removed; repeated delete behavior needs more oracle coverage.

features.name: START
features.notes: START requires indexed/relative access support.

features.name: INVALID KEY / NOT INVALID KEY
features.notes: Current mutation slice supports statement-specific branches for covered file operations.

features.name: RERUN checkpoint/restart
features.notes: A narrow `RERUN ON file EVERY n RECORDS OF file` checkpoint snapshot slice exists; unsupported RERUN shapes remain fail-closed.

features.name: Tape files and EXTEND tape behavior
features.notes: A narrow tape checkpoint backend exists; full tape positioning, EXTEND, parity, and platform behavior remain blocked.

features.name: LINAGE, report/printer controls, ADVANCING PAGE/LINES
features.notes: Selected LINAGE and ADVANCING output bytes are covered; report writer, carriage control tape, and full printer semantics remain blocked.

features.name: DISPLAY
features.notes: Core observable output path is executable.

features.name: ACCEPT
features.notes: ACCEPT is still blocked for executable conversion.

features.name: MOVE
features.notes: Supported for covered storage categories; figurative constants and dialect numeric/editing cases remain partial.

features.name: MOVE CORRESPONDING
features.notes: Blocked instead of silently emitting fallback moves.

features.name: ADD / SUBTRACT / MULTIPLY / DIVIDE
features.notes: Basic decimal arithmetic is executable for covered categories.

features.name: COMPUTE
features.notes: Simple expressions and typed size-error branch slices are covered; functions/exponentiation/multiple targets remain blocked.

features.name: ON SIZE ERROR / NOT ON SIZE ERROR
features.notes: Typed branch bodies are covered for current arithmetic; STRING/UNSTRING overflow branches use the same typed imperative-list path.

features.name: ROUNDED
features.notes: ROUNDED remains blocked in arithmetic lowering.

features.name: IF / ELSE / END-IF
features.notes: Condition IR covers common relations/classes/sign tests; all nested/abbreviated edge cases remain partial.

features.name: EVALUATE
features.notes: Selected subjects, WHEN/WHEN OTHER arms, ALSO patterns, and typed arm statement lists are covered; complex subject and pattern grammar needs more coverage.

features.name: NEXT SENTENCE
features.notes: CFG targets are modeled in this slice, including nested conditional occurrences, but execution remains fail-closed; codegen invariants prevent generated Noop fallback until VM lowering has complete period-scope semantics.

features.name: PERFORM paragraph/range/times/until
features.notes: Common PERFORM forms are executable; complex VARYING AFTER forms remain blocked.

features.name: PERFORM VARYING ... AFTER
features.notes: Nested varying-after loop shape is blocked.

features.name: GO TO
features.notes: Static paragraph GO TO is executable.

features.name: GO TO DEPENDING ON
features.notes: Computed paragraph dispatch is covered in smoke and oracle tests.

features.name: ALTER
features.notes: Current VM supports ALTER in the paragraph-resolution model; overlay-aware targets remain future work.

features.name: Static CALL
features.notes: Linked multi-program CALL is supported; separately loaded runtime programs are not.

features.name: Dynamic CALL
features.notes: Dynamic CALL dispatches among linked generated programs.

features.name: CALL USING BY REFERENCE
features.notes: Default/reference linkage is covered for current structural layouts.

features.name: CALL USING BY CONTENT
features.notes: Blocked until argument conversion and lifecycle semantics are complete.

features.name: CALL USING BY VALUE
features.notes: Blocked in current CALL mode validation.

features.name: GOBACK and STOP RUN
features.notes: Supported in linked program lifecycle subset.

features.name: USE AFTER ERROR ON file
features.notes: File-specific error declaratives are executable for current file runtime events.

features.name: USE FOR DEBUGGING
features.notes: Debugging declarative slice exists; full DEBUG-ITEM and dialect edge cases remain partial.

features.name: READY TRACE / RESET TRACE
features.notes: Basic trace ops exist; full mainframe trace/debug behavior is not complete.

features.name: INSPECT
features.notes: TALLYING ALL, REPLACING ALL, and CONVERTING slices use typed procedure AST lowering; BEFORE/AFTER, LEADING, FIRST, and mixed complex chains remain blocked.

features.name: EXAMINE
features.notes: TALLYING ALL and REPLACING ALL slices use typed procedure AST lowering.

features.name: STRING
features.notes: Delimited by SIZE, pointer, count, and typed overflow branches exist; complex delimiter chains remain partial.

features.name: UNSTRING
features.notes: Core delimiter/target/count and typed overflow branch slices exist; full phrase coverage is partial.

features.name: SEARCH serial
features.notes: Serial SEARCH is executable for supported OCCURS/index forms with typed AT END and WHEN statement lists.

features.name: SEARCH ALL
features.notes: Narrow equality/key forms are supported with typed AT END and found statement lists; unsupported forms block.

features.name: SET index
features.notes: Index definition and SET for current table access are covered.

features.name: Procedure-based SORT with INPUT/OUTPUT PROCEDURE, RELEASE, RETURN
features.notes: In-memory fixed-length SD, one alphanumeric/numeric-display/COMP-3 key, typed callbacks, RELEASE, RETURN, and nested state stack are supported.

features.name: SORT USING / GIVING
features.notes: Physical sort files and external sort utilities are blocked.

features.name: MERGE
features.notes: MERGE remains blocked.

features.name: Report writer
features.notes: Report writer is not implemented.

features.name: ENTER assembler/language
features.notes: Inline assembler/language escape is blocked.

features.name: EXEC SQL
features.notes: EXEC SQL is blocked.

features.name: CICS and DLI
features.notes: Online transaction and database runtime integrations are outside scope.

features.name: CLASS conditions
features.notes: Intrinsic class tests exist; SPECIAL-NAMES CLASS declarations remain blocked.

features.name: SIGN conditions
features.notes: Basic sign tests exist for supported numeric categories.

features.name: Abbreviated combined relations
features.notes: Basic expansion exists; dialect and implicit-subject edges need oracle traces.

features.name: DECIMAL-POINT IS COMMA
features.notes: Blocked until dialect numeric parsing/rendering is complete.

features.name: CURRENCY SIGN
features.notes: Blocked.

features.name: SPECIAL-NAMES CLASS declarations
features.notes: Blocked.

features.name: Printer channel names
features.notes: Blocked with report/printer controls.

features.name: SEGMENT-LIMIT overlays
features.notes: Overlay segment manager is not implemented.

features.name: WITH DEBUGGING MODE
features.notes: Basic debugging declarative/trace support exists; full debugging mode semantics remain partial.

features.name: ZERO, SPACE, QUOTE figurative constants
features.notes: Common targets are covered; figurative constants should be byte-pattern based across all storage categories.

features.name: HIGH-VALUE and LOW-VALUE
features.notes: Known inconsistency between string and byte representations remains.

features.name: ALL figurative constant
features.notes: Some text-operation uses exist; full target-length literal semantics remain partial.

features.name: FUNCTION LENGTH
features.notes: Supported in selected forms; parser whitespace edge case was audited.

features.name: FUNCTION ORD and NUMVAL
features.notes: Tiny intrinsic registry; codepage semantics are not complete.

features.name: Other intrinsic functions
features.notes: Unsupported functions should fail closed.

features.name: IBM dialect profile
features.notes: Compiler/runtime profile exists, but many IBM-specific platform semantics remain blocked.

features.name: GnuCOBOL dialect profile
features.notes: Oracle suite uses GnuCOBOL for a small behavior slice.

features.name: Micro Focus / ACU dialect behavior
features.notes: Profile coverage is not enough for migration claims.

features.name: ASCII/EBCDIC collation
features.notes: Comparison tables exist in places, but storage bytes, text display, and collation are not fully separated. Runtime config parses EBCDIC intent but fails closed until dataset translation exists.

features.name: Custom collating sequence
features.notes: Custom collating sequence is not supported.

features.name: JCL file disposition and job environment
features.notes: Structured runtime config models file bindings and disposition intent for supported fixed sequential files only; JCL job control remains blocked.

features.name: Record locking and concurrent file access
features.notes: Not assessed for migration capability.

features.name: Runtime checkpoint/restart snapshot
features.notes: Planned but not implemented as migration-capable RERUN.

features.name: Separately loaded runtime programs
features.notes: Dynamic calls only dispatch among programs linked into the generated binary.

features.name: Generated file/path behavior security posture
features.notes: Security pass covers generated path and dynamic runtime behavior; further hardening recommendations remain.
